title: Sarepta Therapeutics Financial Health Analysis - DCFmodeling Insights
text:
Financial analysis of Sarepta Therapeutics reveals a company in strong cash flow improvement trajectory supported by ELEVIDYS commercial success. The company achieved cash flow positive status for the first time in Q2 2024 and has maintained this momentum.

Cash Position and Liquidity: As of Q1 2026, cash and investments totaled $748.3 million. Operating cash flow became positive in Q2 2024. This represents a dramatic improvement from the company's historical cash burn rate of $300-400 million annually.

Revenue Growth Trajectory: 2023 revenue: ~$1.1 billion. 2024 revenue: $1.8 billion (56% growth). 2025 guidance: $2.9-3.1 billion (60-70% growth). 2026 guidance: $1.2-1.4 billion (revised downward due to ELEVIDYS label restrictions).

Profitability Path: Company achieved GAAP operating income in Q3 and Q4 2024 for the first time. Non-GAAP profitability achieved earlier. Sustained profitability expected in 2025, providing upside to free cash flow generation.

Expense Management: R&D expenses remain elevated but are being carefully managed. SG&A leverage improving as revenue grows. The company discontinued SRP-5051 program in Q3 2024 due to safety concerns, demonstrating disciplined capital allocation.

Debt and Capital Structure: Sarepta maintains a strong balance sheet with minimal debt. The company is self-funding its pipeline and clinical programs without additional financing needs.

Valuation Implications: The company's transition to profitability and cash flow generation provides multiple paths to value creation. Traditional DCF models suggest fair value in the $25-35 range depending on ELEVIDYS peak sales assumptions.
